recent_trades.summary: Adding to Johnson & Johnson on very positive teclistamab survival data and defensive appeal, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: New Phase 1b data for TECVAYLI (teclistamab) showed superior progression-free and overall survival in multiple myeloma, a material catalyst not yet priced in. Sentiment is very positive (+0.53). JNJ is defensive, currently at a slight loss from our recent buy, and the new data justifies increasing weight to 4%. Position sizing remains prudent with a small cash outlay from ORCL proceeds.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting Oracle completely to lock in +17.6% gain amid negative debt concerns from AI data center expansion, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL sentiment is negative (-0.12) with headlines highlighting debt worries tied to AI data center buildout. The stock is up over 17% from our cost basis, and we recently trimmed; fully exiting crystallizes the gain and reallocates capital to higher-conviction names with fresh catalysts.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in AMD to capture upside momentum as bullish analysts highlight its strong positioning in the broader AI battle.
recent_trades.reasoning: With the AI cycle expanding beyond initial data center builds, AMD is receiving positive coverage as a potential major winner in the next phase of hardware deployment. Initiating a 5% position diversifies our semiconductor holdings and positions us for a catch-up trade.

recent_trades.summary: Doubling our Disney allocation to 10% on extremely positive sentiment and news of major studio cost reductions poised to boost profits.
recent_trades.reasoning: Disney currently boasts the highest sentiment score in the universe (+0.71) driven by structural improvements, specifically a 2.8% reduction in film slate costs expected to boost profits by nearly 37%. We are increasing our weight from 5% to 10% to aggressively capitalize on this fundamental catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Broadcom exposure to 12% to capture partial profits and rebalance the portfolio amid slightly cooling news sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom has performed well for us, but recent headlines have introduced mild negative sentiment (-0.12). Reducing our outsized 18% position to a more moderate 12% locks in gains and frees up capital while maintaining core exposure to the AI theme.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating our Amazon position entirely to avoid continued negative sentiment surrounding Blue Origin setbacks and to free up capital.
recent_trades.reasoning: Amazon's tangential exposure to the Blue Origin rocket explosion has created an overhang on its sentiment score. By completely exiting our 4% position, we eliminate this headline risk and generate liquidity for higher-conviction trades.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% JNJ position on superior survival data for TECVAYLI in multiple myeloma and very positive overall sentiment.
recent_trades.reasoning: JNJ shows +0.53 sentiment backed by clear clinical wins (new TECVAYLI data vs standard of care) that appear under-priced in the current quote; no existing position means this is a clean entry at attractive levels with room to run. Healthcare defensive characteristics also provide portfolio balance on a mixed day.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing DIS to 6% target on very positive sentiment around Spider-Man box office potential, studio cost cuts, and bullish analyst views.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS sentiment is +0.71 with multiple supportive headlines on film profitability and 'the force is still strong'; the stock is up modestly intraday with a constructive chart pattern. Raising from the recent 4% trim as new positive catalysts have appeared since the last action.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming AMZN to 4% target on negative sentiment from Anthropic valuation headlines and slight intraday underperformance.
recent_trades.reasoning: AMZN carries -0.11 sentiment with multiple stories comparing it unfavorably to AI pure-plays; current 7.3% portfolio weight is oversized relative to the negative tone and recent sell-off in related tech. Trimming frees capital for higher-conviction ideas without fully exiting the name.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a modest ORCL position as it pushes near a fresh 30-day high, with AI infrastructure demand still outweighing debt-related concerns.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL is one of the cleaner relative-strength charts in the universe today, up strongly intraday and trading just below its 30-day high after a solid 30-day advance. News sentiment is slightly negative due to debt concerns, but those concerns are tied to AI data-center expansion, which also reinforces the demand backdrop; the small 4% starter size respects that mixed setup.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting the remaining tiny QCOM position because Apple modem risk is still the key headline and the residual stake no longer justifies capital or attention.
recent_trades.reasoning: The Apple in-house modem headline remains a direct fundamental overhang on Qualcomm, and the stock is still below its 30-day high with a softer technical posture than our stronger semiconductor holdings. Since the remaining position is only 0.40% of the portfolio after prior trims, a full exit simplifies the book and modestly funds higher-conviction names.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming LLY as the stock sits at the bottom of its 30-day range despite good news, lowering exposure to a name losing near-term price momentum.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY sentiment is positive, but the tape is not confirming it: the stock is down on the day and effectively sitting at its 30-day low, which is weaker than most other portfolio holdings. After a recent add, reducing the weight from 7.85% to 5% frees capital while keeping a meaningful core position in case the positive obesity-drug narrative reasserts itself.

recent_trades.summary: Boosting Johnson & Johnson on superior cancer drug data and defensive appeal at technical support, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: JNJ sits at a 30-day low with very positive sentiment (+0.53) following teclistamab data showing superior survival outcomes. Its defensive healthcare nature and dividend yield make it an attractive hedge in a risk-off environment. We increase weight from 2.94% to 3.5%.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing NextEra Energy position on positive sentiment and acquisition-driven data center growth catalyst, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: NEE shows positive sentiment (+0.38) and is trading near support. The pending Dominion Energy acquisition is expected to unlock data center growth opportunities, providing a strong forward catalyst. We raise weight from 1.98% to 3.0%.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to Duke Energy on very positive sentiment and technical support near 30-day low, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DUK has a very high sentiment score of +0.74, supported by clean energy momentum and hurricane season preparedness. The stock is near its 30-day low, offering a favorable entry for this defensive utility. We increase weight from 1.99% to 3.0% to reflect conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Selling silver position as it hits a 30-day high with negative sentiment; rotating capital into defensive plays.
recent_trades.reasoning: SLV is at its 30-day high with a negative sentiment score of -0.25, and no positive catalysts in sight. Exiting the position locks in a small gain and provides cash for higher-conviction defensive additions.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating small DIS position on very positive sentiment (+0.71) driven by Spider-Man box office expectations and favorable studio profit improvement analysis.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS has the highest sentiment score in today's universe at +0.71. Headlines point to Spider-Man box office potential boosting entertainment stocks, improving studio economics (36.8% profit boost from cost cuts), and a positive Seeking Alpha piece on Disney's content strength. The stock is near 30-day highs with modest +0.6% 30D gain. A small 4% position captures the upside with limited risk.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming LLY from 12% to 8% — stock is today's biggest loser at -1.5%, breaking below recent support despite positive headlines, reducing risk exposure.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY is down 1.5% today and approaching its 30-day low of $1097. While the fundamental story remains strong (Phase 1b results, $1T valuation narrative, Cramer endorsement), the price action is concerning with a clear downtrend visible on the chart. At 12.1% weight it's our second-largest position — trimming to 8% reduces concentration risk while maintaining meaningful exposure to the long-term thesis.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Amazon exposure to 4% to raise capital and sidestep tangential negative sentiment stemming from Blue Origin's recent setbacks.
recent_trades.reasoning: While Amazon's core operations remain largely insulated, the negative headlines surrounding Bezos-affiliated Blue Origin create an unnecessary sentiment drag. Halving our position frees up capital for our higher-conviction trades in energy and entertainment.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Exxon Mobil to a 9% weight as management warnings of near-record low oil inventories suggest an impending price spike.
recent_trades.reasoning: Exxon's direct warning about record low inventories serves as a potent fundamental catalyst that could drive prices significantly higher, regardless of recent fluctuations in the broader market. We are expanding our initial 5% stake to better capture this anticipated upside.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing HON to 10% target on +1.4% intraday gain, positive Cramer commentary on quantum tech, and overall industrials strength.
recent_trades.reasoning: Current 8% weight can be modestly boosted as HON outperforms the mixed market; sentiment +0.18 with defense and hydrogen transition tailwinds, 30D chart holding near highs. Avoids over-concentration while rotating away from weaker tech names.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating 4% DUK position on very positive sentiment from clean energy adoption, storm preparedness funding, and steady +0.5% intraday performance.
recent_trades.reasoning: Utility offers defensive qualities amid geopolitical risks and Iran-related oil supply disruptions; not currently held, 30D chart is constructive above lows, and aggregate news flow (wind energy, investor attention) is strongly bullish (+0.74). Position sized conservatively given low cash balance.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting QCOM entirely on continued negative headlines about Apple's in-house modem chips threatening its largest customer and -2.6% intraday move.
recent_trades.reasoning: Recent trim already occurred but negative sentiment persists (-0.13); technicals show breakdown from 30D highs with no reversal signals. Full exit frees capital for higher-conviction ideas while staying within max 50 positions and daily trade limits.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating FCX as copper-related momentum, positive sentiment, and a push toward the top of its recent range offer a clean cyclical breakout setup, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: FCX has the strongest 30-day trend in the provided universe subset at +1.48%, supportive sentiment at +0.45, and price action pressing near its 30-day high of 66.10. Adding a mid-sized position also improves diversification away from our heavy tech concentration while keeping exposure in a name benefiting from industrial and AI infrastructure demand.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming RTX modestly as it sits below its 30-day high and continues to lag our stronger industrial holdings despite decent defense news, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: RTX has positive sentiment, but technically it remains below the recent high of 179.00 and is showing less momentum than GE, HON, and DE. Since we recently trimmed it and no major new catalyst has changed the setup, this is only a modest reduction to free capital for higher-conviction additions.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM again as Apple modem headlines directly weaken its customer concentration story and the stock still trails stronger semiconductor peers, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: The negative headline flow is directly fundamental rather than market noise, and QCOM remains one of the weakest names in the semiconductor basket on 30-day performance at -2.59%. We already reduced the position earlier, but the thesis impairment persists and the remaining position is small enough to cut further without sacrificing much upside if sentiment reverses.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming Oracle further to lock in a 15% gain amid rising concerns over debt from AI data center expansion.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL has delivered a substantial 15.43% unrealized profit. Negative sentiment (-0.12) and specific news of debt concerns related to its AI buildout suggest caution. Reducing to 1.5% from 2.12% captures gains while maintaining a small position. This follows a prior trim, and the debt narrative is a new catalyst.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to RTX on positive aerospace and defense contracts, including Javelin missile deliveries and Canadian partnership.
recent_trades.reasoning: RTX sentiment is +0.36 with news of first LWCLU delivery and agreements with Airbus/Canadian defense. The stock is down 0.94% from our cost, presenting a dip-buy opportunity. Raising weight from 2.49% to 3% aligns with improving fundamentals.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 2.5% position in Netflix on the acquisition of an AI startup to reshape content economics, a potential game-changer.
recent_trades.reasoning: Netflix bought Affleck AI startup InterPositive to transform content costs, a unique catalyst that could drive long-term efficiency. Stock is flat over 30 days and slightly down today, allowing an attractive entry before the market prices in the strategic shift.

recent_trades.summary: Adding significantly to Disney on extremely positive sentiment from box office potential and cost-cutting, stock near 30-day low offers good entry.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS sentiment is +0.71, headlines highlight Spider-Man box office, strong franchise strength, and industry-wide cost savings boosting profits. Technicals show the stock near the low end of its 30-day range, providing a favorable risk/reward entry. Increasing to 4% weight reflects high conviction.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to JPM from 2% to 3% — Dimon signaling up to $20B acquisition capacity and financials sector edging higher, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Jamie Dimon's $20B acquisition signal is a new catalyst since my last trim. Financial sector sentiment is positive with record M&A expectations for 2026. Stock at $295.84 is near the middle of its 30-day range, not stretched. Small position increase warranted by improved news flow.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to ABBV from 5.1% to 6% on EU label expansion for VENCLYXTO in CLL, providing a regulatory catalyst, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ABBV received EU Commission authorization for expanded VENCLYXTO label, a meaningful commercial catalyst. Stock is up +0.8% today, outperforming the broader market. Sentiment is positive and the stock has +0.79% 30D return showing steady accumulation. Small add to capture the regulatory catalyst momentum.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating ADBE at 4% — strongest performer today at +1.5%, breaking to new 30-day highs with Michael Burry 'fat pitch' endorsement and AI agent positioning, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: ADBE is at a new 30-day high of $250.64 with +1.54% 30D return, showing clear upward momentum. Michael Burry calling it a 'fat pitch' and the AI agent strategy provide fundamental catalysts. Mixed sentiment (-0.18) from the stock photo cannibalization concern is a risk, but the price action is confirming bullish direction.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Exxon Mobil following management warnings of near-record low oil inventories and an impending price spike.
recent_trades.reasoning: Exxon's explicit warning regarding tightening global oil supply and a subsequent price spike provides a strong near-term bullish catalyst. We are adding a 5% weight to capture the upside of constrained energy markets.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a 5% position in Disney driven by highly positive sentiment and news of major studio cost reductions set to significantly boost profitability.
recent_trades.reasoning: Disney exhibits a sector-leading sentiment score of +0.71 on the back of operational efficiencies, with studios expected to boost profits by nearly 37% through film slate cost-cutting. This fundamental improvement warrants a moderate portfolio allocation.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating our Qualcomm position to lock in a 4.4% gain amid negative news that Apple's in-house modem chips threaten its major customer base.
recent_trades.reasoning: Headlines indicate a direct and fundamental threat to Qualcomm's revenue stream as Apple accelerates its in-house modem efforts. Given our current unrealized profit, a full exit is prudent to avoid the negative sentiment fallout.

recent_trades.summary: Adding AMD to 4% target as analysts see it as the biggest winner in the next AI battle with new AI PC launches.
recent_trades.reasoning: Positive sentiment (+0.13) and Acer copilot+ PC news not yet reflected in today's -1.4% move; small existing 1.6% position leaves room to scale to 4% for better AI exposure without concentration risk.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing LLY to 9% target on positive Phase 1b study results, Cramer praise, and its entrenched weight-loss leadership.
recent_trades.reasoning: Sentiment +0.30 with multiple bullish headlines; chart holds near 30D highs despite flat market, current 6.9% weight can comfortably rise to 9% within the 20% cap, adding to a proven compounder with fresh clinical validation.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM to 4% target on headlines highlighting Apple's in-house modem chips threatening its largest customer.
recent_trades.reasoning: Negative sentiment (-0.13) and -1.6% red candle today reinforce the risk; although we added recently on AI coverage, this new competitive threat is a fundamental shift not yet fully discounted, so reducing from 8.3% weight limits downside while keeping a smaller position.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating a small DIS position as very strong sentiment and improving media profitability headlines support a near-range-high setup, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: DIS stands out with the strongest sentiment score in this run and multiple headlines pointing to better studio economics and sustained franchise strength. Price is near the top of its recent range but not extended, making a starter position appropriate; the 3% weight reflects moderate conviction because this is a new entry without existing portfolio exposure.

recent_trades.summary: Adding modestly to LLY on continued positive obesity-drug and valuation leadership headlines with the stock holding near the top of its range, confidence 8/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: LLY has one of the strongest news sets in the book today, including positive study results and continued reinforcement of its weight-loss leadership, with sentiment firmly positive. The stock is only slightly below its 30-day high and has been consolidating constructively rather than breaking down, so a modest add from 6.87% to 8% keeps size disciplined while leaning into a higher-quality trend.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming QCOM as Apple modem headlines directly pressure a key customer relationship while the stock remains below its 30-day high, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: QCOM has negative sentiment today driven by the Apple in-house modem threat, which is a more direct fundamental risk than the broader AI-related positives in the tape. Technically it is still well off its 30-day high of 259.92, and after already trimming recently, reducing the position further to a minimal 1% weight lowers single-name risk without fully abandoning the name.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming META from 6% to 4% — stock continues to drift lower near 30-day lows at $627.76 with -0.55% unrealized loss, confidence 5/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: META has been weak, trading near the bottom of its 30-day range ($626.62-$634.46). Already trimmed from 8% to 6% yesterday. Reducing further to free cash for stronger names. Will re-add if it reclaims $632+.

recent_trades.summary: Adding to AVGO from 6.3% to 8% on strong institutional sentiment (Steve Cohen top pick) and AI infrastructure positioning, confidence 7/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: Multiple positive headlines about AVGO as a top institutional pick and AI infrastructure play. Stock is +0.2% today with positive momentum. 30-day high is $448.58 vs current $445.58 — close to breakout. Adding ~$2,000 worth.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing Broadcom weight to 18% following a wave of bullish coverage citing its pivotal role in the next generation of AI infrastructure.
recent_trades.reasoning: Broadcom is experiencing highly positive news sentiment, being dubbed a top, safe long-term buy for AI infrastructure. Reallocating the capital freed from Oracle into AVGO maximizes our exposure to a high-momentum, high-conviction secular winner while remaining under the 20% concentration limit.

recent_trades.summary: Liquidating Oracle to lock in a 6.8% gain amid negative news sentiment and rising competitive pressures in the enterprise software space.
recent_trades.reasoning: Oracle's sentiment has turned negative, with recent headlines highlighting increasing competition from players like Palantir challenging core enterprise rules. By fully exiting this position, we secure our unrealized profits and free up capital ahead of pending macroeconomic data.

recent_trades.summary: Trimming RTX again as it continues to lag our stronger industrial and tech holdings while sitting below its recent highs, confidence 6/10.
recent_trades.reasoning: RTX is one of the weakest charts in the current book, showing slight negative intraday pressure and still failing to reclaim the upper end of its 30-day range near 179. We already reduced it recently, and the lack of positive news follow-through versus stronger names like GE and tech winners argues for another modest de-risking rather than a full exit.

recent_trades.summary: Exiting ORCL entirely due to negative sentiment, lack of positive catalysts, and mild underperformance versus tech peers.
recent_trades.reasoning: ORCL headlines are unrelated or negative (Palantir competition, AI speed concerns); chart shows recent red close near lows. Position is only 3.11% so full exit is clean reallocation; no recent trades on it so this is not redundant.

recent_trades.summary: Increasing AVGO to 8% target on positive AI infrastructure headlines, safe-stock status, and +0.2% intraday gain with green candle.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has the strongest sentiment and relevant news among covered names; technicals show steady uptrend within 30D range. Current 4.15% weight allows room to add without concentration risk; this rotates capital from weaker names while respecting recent trim.

recent_trades.summary: Initiating AVGO on strong AI infrastructure positioning, positive sentiment (+0.21), and multiple analyst endorsements including top Steve Cohen stock designation.
recent_trades.reasoning: AVGO has strong positive sentiment (+0.21) with headlines highlighting its positioning for the next big shift in AI infrastructure, endorsement as a top Steve Cohen stock, and designation as a safe long-term buy. The stock is near its 30-day high. With 6.3% cash available and room for a new position, initiating at 6% target weight provides meaningful AI infrastructure diversification alongside NVDA. Markets are at record highs supporting continued tech momentum.

universe.description: 79-name universe: 75 top U.S. mega/large-cap stocks with full GICS sector coverage plus 4 commodity ETFs (gold, silver, oil, copper).

claude._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

gemini._pin_status: UNPINNED: Google echoes the alias unchanged. The adapter now captures response.model_version, so the real dated id will surface in model_id_returned on the next live run; pin from that.

deepseek._pin_note: Deliberately repointed deepseek-v4-flash -> deepseek-v4-pro on 2026-05-21: the deepseek-reasoner alias deprecates 2026-07-24 and maps to sub-frontier v4-flash; v4-pro is DeepSeek's current frontier reasoning tier, restoring cohort parity. No dated immutable snapshot exists beneath deepseek-v4-pro, so this is the most specific callable id (same standard as the dateless Claude ids); residual drift risk if DeepSeek repoints it. Runs in thinking mode at reasoning_effort=high (see deepseek_adapter.py).

claude_opus._pin_status: UNPINNED: Anthropic echoes the alias unchanged; current dated snapshot id unconfirmed. Resolve manually before pinning (a wrong id silently changes the running model).

market_brief.brief: Welcome. U.S. equities: S&P +0.34%, Nasdaq +0.29%, Dow +0.78%. Technology led sectors (+2.89%) while Consumer Staples lagged (-2.31%). GPT-5.4 led the day at +1.14%. 6 of 6 models green on the day. Cumulative leader: DeepSeek V4 Pro at +22.79%. Key headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due - Investor's Business Daily — broadly positive for equities.
market_brief.key_moves: CRM +9.2% (held by 0/6) | ORCL +8.1% (held by 1/6) | ADBE +5.9% (held by 1/6)
market_brief.macro_sentence: Key headline: Dow Jones Futures Fall, Snowflake Surges On Earnings; Fed Inflation Data Due - Investor's Business Daily — broadly positive for equities.
